title: centaurea alba subsp. epapposa
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. epapposa, also known as a white knapweed, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea alba subsp. epapposa include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the individual and the specific preparation method.
